• Returns the three-letter month abbreviation corresponding to the date
new_summary.high_unstructured: The formatDateString function is designed to process ISO date-time strings by extracting the date component, parsing out the month number, and returning the corresponding three-letter month abbreviation (e.g., 'Jan', 'Feb'). It achieves this by splitting the input string at the 'T' character, further splitting the date portion to isolate the month, converting it to an integer, and then indexing into a predefined array of month abbreviations. This function is useful for displaying dates as month abbreviations rather than full dates. It assumes the input string will always contain a 'T' character and that the date is in 'YYYY-MM-DD' format.

  ◦ The 'T' separates the date from the time in ISO date-time strings

  ◦ Extracts the month number from the date
• Converts the month number to an integer
• Uses the month number to index into an array of three-letter month abbreviations
• Returns the corresponding month abbreviation (e.g., 'Jan', 'Feb')
• Useful for formatting or displaying dates as month abbreviations
• Assumes the input string contains a 'T' separator and follows 'YYYY-MM-DD' format
• Does not handle cases where 'T' is missing, the format is different, or the month is out of range

summary.low_structured:
• React component for displaying a stock chart
• Uses Recharts for chart rendering
• Shows company and ticker information
summary.high_unstructured: This code defines a React functional component called StockChart that visualizes stock closing prices using the Recharts library. It first checks if the provided data prop is valid and non-empty, returning null if not. The component extracts the company name and ticker, and includes a helper function to format date strings into month abbreviations (though this function is not used in the rendered output). It maps the stock data into a format suitable for Recharts and renders a responsive line chart with axes, tooltip, legend, and a monotone line representing the price, along with a heading displaying the company and ticker.
summary.medium_structured:
• Receives stock data as a prop
• Checks for valid data before rendering
• Displays company name and ticker
• Formats and maps data for charting
• Renders a responsive line chart with axes, tooltip, and legend
summary.medium_unstructured: The StockChart component takes stock data as a prop and renders a line chart of closing prices using Recharts. It displays the company name and ticker, and processes the data for visualization. If the data is missing or empty, it returns nothing.
summary.high_structured:
• Defines a functional React component named StockChart
• Validates the input data prop:
  ◦ Returns null if data is missing or empty
• Extracts company name and ticker symbol from the data
• Contains a helper function to format date strings into month abbreviations (currently unused in rendering)
• Transforms the stock data into an array of objects with date and closing price for charting
• Renders a styled div containing:
  ◦ A heading with company name and ticker
  ◦ A responsive Recharts LineChart:
    ▪ XAxis for dates
    ▪ YAxis with automatic domain
    ▪ Tooltip and Legend for interactivity
    ▪ A monotone Line displaying the price without dots

summary.low_structured:
• Handles GET requests to /api/stock/:symbol
• Returns stock data using mock or real data
• Responds with JSON
summary.high_unstructured: This code defines an asynchronous Express route at '/api/stock/:symbol' that retrieves stock data for a specified symbol. It first converts the symbol to uppercase and checks if mock data should be used; if so, it generates and returns mock stock data. If not, it fetches historical daily stock data from Yahoo Finance starting from January 1, 2024, extracts relevant fields (date, close, volume), and constructs a response object including the ticker, company name, and data array. The response is logged and sent as JSON, and any errors encountered during processing are logged and result in a 500 error response.
summary.medium_structured:
• Handles GET requests at /api/stock/:symbol
• Determines the stock symbol from the request and converts it to uppercase
• Provides data using one of two methods:
  ◦ Returns mock data if USE_MOCK is true
  ◦ Fetches real data from Yahoo Finance otherwise
• Formats the response with ticker, name, and an array of date, close, and volume
• Handles errors by logging and sending a 500 response
summary.medium_unstructured: This Express route handles GET requests to '/api/stock/:symbol' and responds with stock data for the requested symbol. It either generates mock data or fetches real data from Yahoo Finance, then formats and returns the results as JSON. Errors are caught and result in a 500 status response.
summary.high_structured:
• Defines an async GET route at /api/stock/:symbol
• Extracts and uppercases the stock symbol from the request parameters
• Determines data source:
  ◦ If USE_MOCK is true, generates and returns mock stock data using generateMockData
  ◦ Otherwise, fetches historical daily stock data from Yahoo Finance starting from 2024-01-01
• Processes fetched data:
  ◦ Extracts quotes and company name (using meta.shortName if available)
  ◦ Maps quotes to an array of objects with date, close, and volume
  ◦ Constructs a response object with ticker, name, and data array
• Logs the response to the console
• Sends the response as JSON to the client
• Handles errors by logging them and returning a 500 status with an error message

summaryData.low_structured:

summaryData.high_unstructured: This code defines a StockChart React component that renders a responsive line chart of stock closing prices using the Recharts library. It first validates the input data, extracts company name, ticker, and current price, and includes a helper function to format date strings into month abbreviations for the x-axis. The component maps the provided data into a suitable format for Recharts, then displays the chart with axes, legend, and tooltip, omitting data points for a cleaner look. The chart is wrapped in a styled div and includes a heading with the company name and ticker symbol. Additionally, the chart now features both a ReferenceLine component and a separate orange Line component that visually highlight the current price with a labeled, dashed orange line and a distinct orange dot at the latest data point.
